_note: MOCK procedure result. No real patient. For PreChart development/eval.

procedure.name: Endoscopic submucosal dissection (ESD), gastric antrum
procedure.indication: Gastric antral adenoma with biopsy-proven high-grade dysplasia; EUS without submucosal invasion — en-bloc endoscopic resection
procedure.sedation: General anesthesia with endotracheal intubation for airway protection
procedure.prep_quality: N/A (upper GI); stomach adequately emptied after overnight fast
procedure.extent_reached: Gastric antrum lesion resected; scope advanced to second portion of duodenum, remainder of stomach and esophagus surveyed

procedure_report: PROCEDURE: Endoscopic submucosal dissection (ESD) of gastric antral lesion. INDICATION: 25 mm flat, slightly depressed gastric antral lesion with biopsy-proven high-grade dysplasia; staging EUS without submucosal invasion or perigastric lymphadenopathy. ANTITHROMBOTIC HOLD: Low-dose aspirin (primary prevention) held per high-bleed-risk resection protocol without cardiology clearance; OTC fish oil (omega-3) and OTC ibuprofen held pre-procedure; amlodipine continued through morning of procedure. Pre-procedure labs acceptable (Hgb 13.9, plt 244, INR 1.0). SEDATION: general anesthesia with endotracheal intubation. FINDINGS: The esophagus and gastroesophageal junction were normal. In the gastric antrum, the previously marked 25 mm flat, slightly depressed lesion with abnormal narrow-band-imaging surface pattern was identified. Circumferential marking dots were placed 5 mm outside the lesion margin. Submucosal injection of a lifting solution with indigo carmine produced a good lift with no non-lifting sign. Circumferential mucosal incision followed by submucosal dissection was performed, achieving en-bloc resection of the lesion on a single specimen. The resection bed was inspected; several visible vessels were coagulated prophylactically with coagulation forceps. No perforation. No active bleeding at completion. The specimen was retrieved, pinned, and sent to pathology for en-bloc margin assessment. The remainder of the stomach and the examined duodenum were normal. IMPRESSION: Successful en-bloc ESD of gastric antral high-grade dysplasia lesion; no immediate complication. Pathology pending for depth of invasion and lateral/deep margin status.

findings.finding: 25 mm flat, slightly depressed antral lesion with abnormal NBI surface pattern, resected en-bloc
findings.action_taken: endoscopic submucosal dissection, en-bloc, specimen retrieved

findings.finding: Resection bed with visible vessels, no active bleeding
Normal esophagus, remaining stomach, and examined duodenum

disposition: Admitted for overnight observation per high-bleed-risk gastric ESD protocol. NPO overnight, IV proton pump inhibitor, advance to clear liquids in AM if stable. Delayed-bleeding and perforation precautions reviewed.

followup.recommendation: Review ESD specimen pathology (depth of invasion, lateral and deep margin status, lymphovascular invasion) to determine curative vs non-curative resection and need for further therapy
Delayed post-ESD bleeding watch — counsel on melena/hematemesis; high-bleed-risk gastric resection
Resume held medications (aspirin, fish oil, ibuprofen) only after confirmed hemostasis per post-ESD protocol; reassess NSAID need with PCP
Continue high-dose PPI to promote artificial ulcer healing; plan surveillance EGD
Add amlodipine 5 mg daily to reconciled medication list; continue for hypertension

planted_followup_items.description: ESD en-bloc specimen pathology pending — depth of invasion and lateral/deep margin status determine whether resection was curative; a positive deep margin or unsuspected submucosal invasion would upstage to carcinoma and trigger oncology/surgical referral
planted_followup_items.expected_action: review margins and depth on final pathology, communicate result to patient, escalate to multidisciplinary/surgical review if non-curative

planted_followup_items.description: Delayed post-ESD bleeding and perforation watch after high-bleed-risk gastric antral resection
planted_followup_items.expected_action: counsel patient on melena/hematemesis/abdominal pain; ensure access to care; assess before restarting any antithrombotic/NSAID

planted_followup_items.description: Held aspirin (primary prevention, antiplatelet) plus OTC fish oil and OTC ibuprofen must be resumed only after confirmed hemostasis; NSAID need should be reassessed given post-ESD ulcer
planted_followup_items.expected_action: confirm hemostasis then give explicit resume-or-hold instructions; coordinate NSAID alternative with PCP

planted_followup_items.description: Surveillance EGD to assess artificial ulcer healing and exclude residual/recurrent dysplasia
planted_followup_items.expected_action: schedule surveillance EGD in 3-6 months on high-dose PPI

planted_followup_items.description: Post-resection gastric neoplasia surveillance interval to be set once curative status confirmed by pathology
planted_followup_items.expected_action: document surveillance interval after final pathology

planted_followup_items.description: Medication reconciliation gap: amlodipine 5 mg daily taken but absent from chart medication list; undocumented OTC ibuprofen also identified
planted_followup_items.expected_action: add amlodipine and OTC ibuprofen to reconciled medication list; continue amlodipine for hypertension

post_procedure_followup._note: Patient-reported stream after the procedure — the inbound signal the LeftBehind product triages. Each contact is labeled ground truth so triage can be scored.

contacts.verbatim: My throat is a bit scratchy from the breathing tube and my upper belly feels sore where they worked. Nothing bad, just achy.
contacts.clinical_signal: Expected post-intubation sore throat and mild epigastric soreness at the ESD resection site; no bleeding or systemic signs
contacts.expected_action: Reassure; sore throat from intubation and mild epigastric discomfort after ESD are expected and self-limited. Advise soft diet, prescribed PPI, and to call for melena, hematemesis, severe/worsening pain, or fever.
contacts.rationale: Benign expected symptoms after ESD under general anesthesia; no red-flag features.

contacts.verbatim: My knee was killing me so I went back on my ibuprofen yesterday, and I restarted the baby aspirin too since I felt fine. Is that okay?
contacts.clinical_signal: TRIAGE TRAP (med-timing confusion): patient self-resumed NSAID and antiplatelet on day 2 after a high-bleed-risk gastric ESD, before hemostasis window per protocol — raises delayed post-ESD bleeding risk
contacts.expected_action: Escalate to GI same day: instruct patient to STOP the ibuprofen and aspirin immediately, reinforce they must remain held until hemostasis is confirmed per post-ESD protocol, counsel on delayed-bleeding warning signs, and arrange an NSAID alternative (acetaminophen) for knee pain with PCP.
contacts.rationale: Early self-resumption of an NSAID plus an antiplatelet after high-bleed-risk ESD is a live bleeding hazard; benign-sounding question but requires prompt clinical counseling, so it escalates.

contacts.verbatim: I've been sticking to soft foods. When can I eat normally again and go back to my usual meals?
contacts.clinical_signal: Routine diet-advancement question; no symptoms of bleeding or perforation reported
contacts.expected_action: Answer from discharge instructions: advance diet gradually per post-ESD protocol while continuing high-dose PPI; return precautions for melena, hematemesis, or severe pain reviewed.
contacts.rationale: Answerable informational question with no clinical red flags.

contacts.verbatim: It's been almost two weeks and no one ever called me about the results from the tissue they removed. I'm anxious about whether it was cancer. Did something get lost?
contacts.clinical_signal: DROPPED BALL: pending ESD pathology (curative vs non-curative margins/depth) never communicated to the patient within the expected window
contacts.expected_action: Close the loop: retrieve the ESD margin/depth pathology, have GI call the patient with the result, and schedule follow-up to review curative status and surveillance interval; if non-curative, arrange multidisciplinary/surgical referral.
contacts.rationale: Flags a dropped pathology follow-up on the critical pending_pathology item (F1); the result must be located and communicated, and follow-up scheduled.
